prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF backend, density-fitted) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz (no re-optimization). All SCFs converged; integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true); sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported.

Most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (N), f⁻ = 0.20452359 (headline value reported).
Most electrophilic site: Atom 3 (C, ortho-carbon), f⁺ = 2.2975867.

Note: Mulliken-based Fukui indices with large basis sets (def2-TZVP) can yield values outside [0,1] and negative per-atom values — a known artefact of Mulliken partitioning. Relative site ordering is more physically meaningful than absolute magnitudes.
